summary:
Polysulfide impression material shows poorer durability because of chemical instability caused primarily through absorption properties making it less suitable than others when extended periods between mixing & application are needed; whereas more modern additions like polyethers provide enhanced resilience against degradation factors extending overall usability beyond typical expectations set forth initially by earlier generation products including polysulphides themselves.
The primary reason is not just about physical characteristics but also how well each type resists deterioration under real-world conditions where precise timing isn't always feasible during dental procedures involving impressions requiring accurate reproduction post-molding stages completed days apart if necessary depending upon patient needs assessed clinically beforehand ensuring optimal
